end of world model.

In inverted mode:
- LEFT: charcoal +3, black -3 (they move toward each other)
- RIGHT: charcoal -3, black +3 (they move away from each other)

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model:
- Back to initial config: charcoal at cols 21-29 (left), black at cols 45-53 (right), yellow at cols 9-17 (left).
- Dots still dark blue. Level not completed after multiple SPACE attempts.
- Mode: inverted (after last SPACE).
- Key insight: charcoal and black are at SAME vertical position (rows 18-29). In inverted mode, LEFT moves them toward each other. After 4 LEFTs, they'd meet at cols ~33-41, overlapping!
- In Level 1, overlapping charcoal+yellow made dots turn yellow. Maybe overlapping charcoal+black in Level 2 triggers the completion.
Plan: Press LEFT 4 times in inverted mode to make charcoal and black overlap, then press SPACE.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current mode: inverted
# LEFT moves charcoal RIGHT (+3) and black LEFT (-3)
# After 4 LEFTs: charcoal 21+12=33, black 45-12=33
# They should overlap!
result = action(['LEFT'] * 4)
print(f"4x LEFT: done={result.get('done')}, level_completed={result.get('level_completed')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] > 50:
        print(f"Charcoal: boundary={n['boundary'][:4]}")
    if n['color'] == 'B' and 50 < n['pixels'] < 100:
        print(f"Black: boundary={n['boundary'][:4]}")
    if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] > 50:
        print(f"Yellow: boundary={n['boundary'][:4]}")

# Check dots
dots = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] <= 5]
y_dots = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] <= 5]
print(f"Dark blue dots: {len(dots)}, Yellow dots: {len(y_dots)}")

# Try SPACE
if not result.get('done'):
    result = action(['SPACE'])
    print(f"SPACE: done={result.get('done')}, level_completed={result.get('level_completed')}")
    
    # Check dots again
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    dots = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] <= 5]
    y_dots = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] <= 5]
    print(f"Dark blue dots: {len(dots)}, Yellow dots: {len(y_dots)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

- In Level 1, overlapping charcoal+yellow made dots turn yellow. Maybe overlapping charcoal+black in Level 2 triggers the completion.
Plan: Press LEFT 4 times in inverted mode to make charcoal and black overlap, then press SPACE.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current mode: inverted
# LEFT moves charcoal RIGHT (+3) and black LEFT (-3)
# After 4 LEFTs: charcoal 21+12=33, black 45-12=33
# They should overlap!
result = action(['LEFT'] * 4)
print(f"4x LEFT: done={result.get('done')}, level_completed={result.get('level_completed')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] > 50:
        print(f"Charcoal: boundary={n['boundary'][:4]}")
    if n['color'] == 'B' and 50 < n['pixels'] < 100:
        print(f"Black: boundary={n['boundary'][:4]}")
    if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] > 50:
        print(f"Yellow: boundary={n['boundary'][:4]}")

# Check dots
dots = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] <= 5]
y_dots = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] <= 5]
print(f"Dark blue dots: {len(dots)}, Yellow dots: {len(y_dots)}")

# Try SPACE
if not result.get('done'):
    result = action(['SPACE'])
    print(f"SPACE: done={result.get('done')}, level_completed={result.get('level_completed')}")
    
    # Check dots again
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    dots = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] <= 5]
    y_dots = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] <= 5]
    print(f"Dark blue dots: {len(dots)}, Yellow dots: {len(y_dots)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
